title: [InstCombine] Inverse is.fpclass mask operand, when profitable

tests.outside_scope_reason: The summary explicitly mentions AND/OR logic reductions but does not mention XOR operations. The patch code handles XOR through the BinaryOperator switch in foldLogicOfIsFPClass, making this an uncovered pattern in the summary text.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @xor_is_fpclass_masks(float %x) {
#0:
  %is.neg = is.fpclass float %x, i32 8
  %is.pos = is.fpclass float %x, i32 4
  %xor = xor i1 %is.neg, %is.pos
  ret i1 %xor
}
=>
define i1 @xor_is_fpclass_masks(float %x) {
#0:
  %is.neg = is.fpclass float %x, i32 12
  ret i1 %is.neg
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: The summary mentions recognizing inverted forms with single not(is.fpclass(...)) but does not discuss double-negation patterns. The foldNot optimization in the patch handles cascaded not operations, which is an uncovered edge case.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @double_negated_is_fpclass(double %x) {
#0:
  %is.class = is.fpclass double %x, i32 511
  %not1 = xor i1 %is.class, 1
  %not2 = xor i1 %not1, 1
  ret i1 %not2
}
=>
define i1 @double_negated_is_fpclass(double %x) {
#0:
  %is.class = fcmp une double %x, inf
  ret i1 %is.class
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

tests.source_program:
define i1 @fcmp_une_nan_or_inverted_is_fpclass(half %x) {
  %is.nan = fcmp une half %x, %x
  %is.finite = call i1 @llvm.is.fpclass.f16(half %x, i32 60)
  %not.finite = xor i1 %is.finite, true
  %or = or i1 %is.nan, %not.finite
  ret i1 %or
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: The summary describes combining fcmp with is.fpclass separately from handling inverted is.fpclass forms, but does not explicitly mention mixing both patterns in a single expression (fcmp combined with not(is.fpclass)). The patch handles this through foldLogicOfIsFPClass recognizing both matchIsFPClassLikeFCmp and IsRHSInverseClass simultaneously.

alive2.src:
define i1 @fcmp_une_nan_or_inverted_is_fpclass(half %x) {
  %is.nan = fcmp une half %x, %x
  %is.finite = call i1 @llvm.is.fpclass.f16(half %x, i32 60)
  %not.finite = xor i1 %is.finite, true
  %or = or i1 %is.nan, %not.finite
  ret i1 %or
}

  %is.nan = fcmp une half %x, %x
  %is.finite = is.fpclass half %x, i32 60
  %not.finite = xor i1 %is.finite, 1
  %or = or i1 %is.nan, %not.finite
  ret i1 %or
}
